title: Research: application lifecycle scoping for coroutine refactor
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

Current state: Investigating whether coroutine scope for ensureBackgroundThread refactor can be tied to Application lifecycle.

Desired state: Clear understanding of Android process lifecycle constraints for coroutine scoping.

Reason: Research findings (2026-04-24):

## Key Finding: No Reliable App Death Callback
- Application.onTerminate() — emulator only, never called on real devices
- ProcessLifecycleOwner — does not dispatch ON_DESTROY for the process
- OS SIGKILLs the process — all threads/coroutines die regardless
- Therefore: process-lifetime SupervisorJob and top-level private val achieve the same result

## Implications
- A top-level SupervisorJob scope in commons is effectively app-scoped already
- No benefit to involving Application class purely for lifecycle cancellation
- Real lifecycle cancellation requires per-Activity/Fragment scopes (app-level change)

## Pragmatic Baseline
- Commons: top-level SupervisorJob + Dispatchers.IO (process-scoped, honest about what it is)
- Apps can layer per-component scopes as follow-up without further commons changes
- Semantic clarity over architectural purity — the OS kills everything anyway

## When Application Scope WOULD Help
- If commons provided an explicit BackgroundScope interface that apps could wire to their Application
- For shared state initialization (e.g., shared CoroutineExceptionHandler)
- Not for cancellation — that is per-component
